title: Roasted Kenyan Lentil Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful Kenyan-inspired stew made with lentils, allspice, and a hint of mustard, cooked to perfection in a ttukbaegi earthenware pot on a lazy susan spinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the onion and garlic with 1 tablespoon of oil and roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until lightly browned.
In a small pan, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil over medium heat and add the mustard seeds, letting them pop for 1 minute.
Add the allspice and salt to the pan and stir for 1 minute, until fragrant.
In the ttukbaegi earthenware pot, combine the roasted onion and garlic, cooked mustard seed mixture, lentils, and water.
Bring the stew to a boil on the stovetop, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
Use the lazy susan spinner to rotate the pot and ensure even cooking.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
